title: In conversation: Dave Murphy and Tom Murphy – can modernity survive planetary limits?
content: In a wide-ranging exchange, physicist Tom Murphy and energy scholar Dave Murphy explore the tension between optimism and planetary limits, debating whether modernity can endure or must give way to something entirely new.

creator: Tom Murphy
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Ultra-processed information: AI and the coming deluge of noise
content: Nate Hagens explores the growing sense that many people feel disoriented and overwhelmed in a world increasingly saturated with digital content.
snippet: Nate Hagens explores the growing sense that many people feel disoriented and overwhelmed in a world increasingly saturated with digital content.
creator: Nate Hagens
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Human Nature Odyssey, Episode 21. Earth Abides (Part 2): Future Animists
content: It’s been fifty years since the collapse of civilization - why isn’t everything back up and running already? In the sci-fi novel "Earth Abides," Ish tries to teach the next generation about the old ways but these dang kids would rather explore abandoned boulevards and overgrown shopping malls.

creator: Alex Leff
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The UK can’t debate its way out of climate impacts. It needs a Plan B now
content: As climate impacts intensify, the UK remains dangerously unprepared for systemic shocks, from global heating to biodiversity collapse. Instead of waiting for consensus on long-term solutions, the focus must shift to resilience.
snippet: As climate impacts intensify, the UK remains dangerously unprepared for systemic shocks, from global heating to biodiversity collapse. Instead of waiting for consensus on long-term solutions, the focu
creator: Theo Cox
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Corporations have become the world’s most powerful institutions. It’s time to rewrite the rules
content: From engineered consumer addiction to environmental destruction, corporate harm is not a failure of the system but its logic. But because corporations exist by public charter, that logic can be rewritten through democratic oversight, time-limited licenses and rules that focus on risks to people and the planet.
snippet: From engineered consumer addiction to environmental destruction, corporate harm is not a failure of the system but its logic. But because corporations exist by public charter, that logic can be rewrit
creator: Jeremy Lent
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Crazy Town, Episode 123 Mailbag: The Crazy Townies Speak!
content: It’s really refreshing to hear from you, our listeners and fellow strugglers living in high-energy modernity (affectionately known as Crazy Townies). This mailbag episode offers the element of surprise, as it gives us a chance to respond with delight and spontaneity to your questions and comments.

creator: Asher Miller
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: In the Rising Tide, Episode 1. Louise Mabulo: The chef who grew a forest
content: Our journey begins in the Philippines with farmer, chef, entrepreneur, and environmentalist Louise Mabulo. From her early start on MasterChef Junior to founding The Culinary Lounge and The Cacao Project, Louise works to reconnect people with the land—and the food they grow and eat.
snippet: Our journey begins in the Philippines with farmer, chef, entrepreneur, and environmentalist Louise Mabulo. From her early start on MasterChef Junior to founding The Culinary Lounge and The Cacao Proje
creator: Alex Leff
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Human Exceptionalism: How rethinking our place in the web of life could change our global crises
content: In this episode, Nate speaks with primatologist and author Dr. Christine Webb about human exceptionalism – the deeply embedded belief that humans are separate from and superior to the rest of nature. Webb argues this worldview is not a universal human trait but rather a product of a few dominant cultures, and that it lies at the root of many of our most pressing global challenges.
snippet: In this episode, Nate speaks with primatologist and author Dr. Christine Webb about human exceptionalism – the deeply embedded belief that humans are separate from and superior to the rest of nature.
creator: Nate Hagens
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: In the Rising Tide, Episode 2. Hajar Tazi: Weaving Our Way Back Home
content: We travel to Morocco to speak with storyteller and ecosystem weaver Hajar Tazi. Working across a wide network of organizations—from Shareable and Gaia Education and the Wellbeing Economy Alliance—Hajar helps connect people, ideas, and communities in service of a more resilient future.
snippet: We travel to Morocco to speak with storyteller and ecosystem weaver Hajar Tazi. Working across a wide network of organizations—from Shareable and Gaia Education and the Wellbeing Economy Alliance—Haja
creator: Alex Leff
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: How forests act as natural flood defences: New research shows trees can slash flood risk
content: New research finds that healthy forests act as natural infrastructure that can significantly reduce the frequency and severity of floods, both small and large.
snippet: New research finds that healthy forests act as natural infrastructure that can significantly reduce the frequency and severity of floods, both small and large.
creator: Samadhee Kaluarachchi
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Beyond Growth: Building a wellbeing economy can help us avoid collapse
content: As the planet strains under endless GDP growth, econometrician Gaya Herrington makes the case for a “wellbeing economy” that trades our obsession with more for a future of enough: redirecting innovation, work and policy toward human flourishing and healthy ecosystems within the Earth’s limits.
snippet: As the planet strains under endless GDP growth, econometrician Gaya Herrington makes the case for a “wellbeing economy” that trades our obsession with more for a future of enough: redirecting innovati
creator: Gaya Herrington
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: What to do as the world falls apart: A framework for action
content: From the archive | Having spent twenty years articulating the more-than-human predicament, Nate Hagens shares a timely first-pass framework for action and response organized around what to do now, which could be applied in various contexts and at multiple scales.

creator: Nate Hagens

title: Across Africa, farmers are adopting regenerative agricultural practices that support food sovereignty amid global instability
content: As geopolitical tensions drive up resource costs and disrupt supply, fertilizer prices may soar, endangering farming systems dependent on imports. Regenerative solutions, like green manure and community finance, are expanding across African countries, restoring local control over food systems.

creator: Kate Schecter

title: On Natural Capital: The Value of the World Around Us by Partha Dasgupta – review
content: In On Natural Capital, Partha Dasgupta argues that mainstream economics has failed to recognize the finite nature of the natural world. Blending ecology and economics, he sets out a framework to account for our impact on the environment.
snippet: In On Natural Capital, Partha Dasgupta argues that mainstream economics has failed to recognize the finite nature of the natural world. Blending ecology and economics, he sets out a framework to accou
creator: Jacob Lea
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Bill Rees: A childhood moment on a Canadian farm led to ecological footprint analysis
content: A childhood moment on a Canadian farm shaped Bill Rees’s understanding of ecological limits, leading to the development of ecological footprint analysis and decades of warnings about global overshoot.
snippet: A childhood moment on a Canadian farm shaped Bill Rees’s understanding of ecological limits, leading to the development of ecological footprint analysis and decades of warnings about global overshoot.
creator: Robert Jensen
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.
